commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can preserve multi-value response headers in the wrapper; maintainer security/ops review still remains before merge.; Needs attention: The diff changes the public API rate-limit security boundary and currently has an auth-cookie preservation risk in the wrapper.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve multi-value response headers when wrapping routes — convex/lib/httpRouteRateLimit.ts:103-106 (sha=da59852b29f531b03ce05bdbeb987a2a6592ed45)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Convex HTTP route-registration wrapper for central rate-limit policy, changes anonymous client-IP and counter-sharding behavior, and updates rate-limit docs, specs, and tests.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main registers raw HTTP routes and handler-level limits, while the PR wraps route registration and tests denied legacy/package behavior; I did not run a live Convex deployment.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can preserve multi-value response headers in the wrapper; maintainer security/ops review still remains before merge.

target.title: Harden API rate limiting
target.body:
## Summary

This PR hardens ClawHub HTTP API rate limiting and makes HTTP route coverage systemic. In the installed Convex `httpRouter` surface, the route boundary is `http.route(...)`; there is no first-class middleware hook. The PR therefore installs one route-registration wrapper immediately after `httpRouter()` and before any app or auth routes are added.

The wrapper is now the authoritative HTTP boundary. It applies a central policy instead of keeping a second inventory of handler-limited routes:

- `OPTIONS` preflights and auth metadata/JWKS discovery are intentionally unmetered.
- `GET` defaults to the `read` bucket.
- Non-`OPTIONS` mutations default to the `write` bucket.
- Downloads, exports, trusted publish token minting, package artifact reads, and npm tarballs are classified into their specialized buckets centrally.
- Existing handler-level `applyRateLimit(...)` calls become compatibility no-ops when the wrapper has already metered the same `Request`, so routes do not double-consume quota while the central policy remains authoritative.

It also closes bypass classes where legacy API handlers, forwarded client IP headers, and rate-limit counter shards could let traffic avoid or multiply quota enforcement. The intended contract is documented in `specs/rate-limiting.md`, `docs/http-api.md`, and the deploy notes for `TRUST_FORWARDED_IPS`.

## What Changed

- Router-level HTTP coverage: `convex/http.ts` uses `installRateLimitedRoutes(httpRouter())`; all later `http.route(...)` calls, including auth routes added by `auth.addHttpRoutes(http)`, are covered by default.
- Central route policy: `convex/lib/httpRouteRateLimit.ts` owns bucket selection by method and route family, with only intentional opt-outs for preflight and auth metadata.
- Handler compatibility: `convex/lib/httpRateLimit.ts` tracks wrapper-metered `Request` objects so old in-handler limit calls return the same headers without consuming another counter.
- Legacy API routes: `/api/...` and `/api/cli/...` routes now rate-limit through the router wrapper before auth parsing, search work, upload ticket creation, publish/delete mutations, and telemetry writes.
- Package downloads: `/api/v1/packages/{name}/versions/{version}/download`, package artifacts, npm tarballs, and legacy download endpoints consume the `download` bucket.
- Client IP trust: anonymous traffic no longer trusts `cf-connecting-ip`, `x-forwarded-for`, `x-real-ip`, or `fly-client-ip` unless `TRUST_FORWARDED_IPS` is explicitly enabled.
- Missing-IP fallback buckets: anonymous requests without a trusted client IP use bounded route-family scopes, not user-controlled slugs, package names, versions, or artifact parameters.
- Sharded counters: each shard has a partition of the configured quota, so sharding no longer acts as a quota multiplier. Transient counter write conflicts are retried and then fail closed with `503` if the limiter cannot safely consume.
## Proof

Behavioral proof:

- Package version downloads consume the `download` bucket. A request shaped like this, using any public package/version fixture, now consumes the download policy:

```http
GET /api/v1/packages/demo-plugin/versions/1.0.0/download HTTP/1.1
Host: clawhub.ai
```

If the caller has exhausted the download quota, the expected response is:

```http
HTTP/1.1 429 Too Many Requests
Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8
RateLimit-Limit: 1200
RateLimit-Remaining: 0
Retry-After: <seconds>

Rate limit exceeded
```

- Legacy CLI routes are limited before route-specific work. For example, an exhausted caller hitting the legacy publish endpoint receives `429` before the handler attempts to parse auth or publish payloads:

```http
POST /api/cli/publish HTTP/1.1
Host: clawhub.ai
Content-Type: application/json

{
```

Expected response:

```http
HTTP/1.1 429 Too Many Requests

Rate limit exceeded
```

- Existing v1 handlers that still call `applyRateLimit(...)` do not double-consume. The wrapper meters once, marks the `Request`, and nested handler checks return the wrapper headers.
- Auth sign-in callback routes are covered by the router-level default because the installer runs before `auth.addHttpRoutes(http)`. Auth metadata/JWKS routes are intentionally exempted.
- Missing trusted IP headers no longer create per-artifact fallback buckets. Requests such as `/api/v1/packages/first-plugin` and `/api/v1/packages/second-plugin` share the same bounded anonymous route-family key for the `read` bucket; caller-controlled package names and versions are not part of the fallback key.
- Counter shards can spread write contention, but they cannot multiply the public quota. Once a shard reaches its partition capacity, consumption tries another active shard or returns the current limiter status instead of allowing extra quota.

## Verification

Reviewer-checkable behavior:

- `convex/lib/httpRouteRateLimit.test.ts` verifies default method policy, central special buckets, prefix classification for package/npm downloads, auth metadata opt-out, central overrides, request marking.
- `convex/http.test.ts` verifies legacy routes are behind the router-level default limiter, auth sign-in routes are covered, package version downloads consume the download limit, and existing internally-limited v1 routes are not double-consumed.
- `convex/lib/httpRateLimit.test.ts` verifies trusted IP headers are ignored by default, missing-IP fallback keys are route-family scoped, and counter write conflicts retry/fail closed.
- `convex/rateLimits.test.ts` verifies shard partition capacity and out-of-range shard normalization.

Automated checks:

- `bunx vitest run convex/http.test.ts convex/lib/httpRouteRateLimit.test.ts convex/httpApi.handlers.test.ts convex/httpApiV1.handlers.test.ts convex/lib/httpRateLimit.test.ts convex/rateLimits.test.ts` passed: 464 tests.
- `VITE_CONVEX_URL=https://example.invalid bun run coverage` passed: 3719 passed, 1 skipped.
- `bunx oxlint --type-aware --tsconfig ./tsconfig.oxlint.json` on the touched rate-limit/HTTP files passed.
- Latest `bun run ci:static` now fails in unrelated React UI files on `typescript/no-redundant-type-constituents`; none of the reported files are touched by this PR. Earlier full static runs on this branch passed before that unrelated lint surface appeared.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `bunx tsc -p packages/schema/tsconfig.json --noEmit` passed.
- `bunx tsc -p packages/clawhub/tsconfig.json --noEmit` passed.

Not passing locally:

- `bun run ci:types-build` fails only on pre-existing `e2e/prod-http-smoke.e2e.test.ts:94` because `lastError` is not defined. That file is not touched by this PR.

Not run:

- Local structured autoreview could not start because the local reviewer engine rejected the auth token with `401 Unauthorized` before producing findings.
target.author: jesse-merhi
